editorial_reasoning.calendar_tier: TIER 2 — NBA playoffs set (day after regular season), WNBA Draft today
editorial_reasoning.dominant_topic: NBA playoffs bracket finalized — major storylines with Doc firing and play-in drama

candidates_considered.story: NBA playoffs bracket set, play-in drama
candidates_considered.hook: Lakers-Rockets first round, De'Aaron Fox choked away Spurs easy path
Third coach in three years, 32-50 season, Giannis questions
Fourth player ever to repeat, joined Tiger/Jack/Faldo

candidates_considered.story: Cooper Flagg led Mavs in all categories
candidates_considered.hook: First rookie since MJ to lead team in PTS/REB/AST/STL

candidates_considered.story: Kon Knueppel beats LaMelo in 3PT race
candidates_considered.hook: Rookie won 3PT title by ONE three over Ball
11-11 FG with 26 PTS, 3 STL, 3 BLK — historic perfection

stories.headline: NBA Playoffs Set: Lakers Draw Rockets, Fox Chokes Away Spurs' Easy Path
stories.editorial_direction: Lead with the bracket being set, then dive into the Fox collapse that screwed the Spurs, Lakers-Rockets without superstars, play-in chaos. This is the meat of the NBA season ending.

tweets.text:
Play-in is set:

West:
PHX (7) vs POR (8)
LAC (9) vs GSW (10)

East:
PHI (7) vs ORL (8)
CHA (9) vs MIA (10)

Who makes it out?
The Spurs had a chance to avoid the Nuggets and the Thunder in the first 2 rounds of the Playoffs and De'Aaron Fox missed 3 STRAIGHT WIDE OPEN corner 3s in less than 10 seconds 😭😭
De'Aaron Fox with a chance to give the Spurs an easy road straight to the Western Conference Finals

key_matchups: Lakers (4) vs Rockets (5) — KD vs Luka-less Lakers
Spurs (2) get winner of 7/8 play-in instead of easier opponent
Thunder (1) locked into top seed, defending champions
fox_collapse: Fox missed 3 straight wide open 3s in final 10 seconds vs Spurs
Sacramento loss meant Spurs couldn't avoid Thunder/Nuggets early
Spurs now face much harder path to conference finals
lakers_drama: Without both Luka and Austin Reaves for playoffs
Face Rockets with KD who 'look to take advantage'
play_in_chaos: West: PHX vs POR for 7-seed, LAC vs GSW elimination game
East: 76ers vs Magic for 7-seed, Hornets vs Heat elimination
Heat tumbled from top-6 to needing two road wins like last year
historical_context: Thunder third straight 1-seed as defending champions
Bucks missed playoffs for first time since 2016
Play-in tournament April 14-17, playoffs start April 18
where_it_goes: Will Lakers survive without superstars vs KD?
Can Spurs overcome harder path after Fox choke?

stories.headline: Doc Rivers Fired: Bucks Begin Third Coach Search in Three Years
stories.editorial_direction: The coaching carousel dysfunction. 32-50 season, Giannis played 36 games, disconnect with players, now what for Milwaukee?

tweets.text: BREAKING: Doc Rivers is departing as head coach of the Milwaukee Bucks, sources tell ESPN. After a 32-50 season, the Bucks will embark on their third coaching search in the last three years.

Doc Rivers is stepping down as Bucks head coach. 

The combination of Doc Rivers and Darvin Ham was an Avengers level threat 😭

the_record: 32-50 season, missed playoffs for first time since 2016
97-103 overall in three seasons with Milwaukee
Two first-round exits, missed play-in this year
coaching_chaos: Third coaching search in three years
Fired Mike Budenholzer in 2023 (won championship in 2021)
Adrian Griffin went 30-13, fired after 43 games for Rivers
player_disconnect: ESPN reports 'season-long disconnect between Rivers and players'
Giannis played only 36 games due to injuries
Rivers blamed lack of success on player injuries each season
contract_details: Rivers has one year left at eight-figure salary
Bucks will pay out full 2026-27 contract
Discussing potential advisory role in organization
hall_of_fame_timing: Just named to Naismith Hall of Fame Class of 2026
Sixth all-time in NBA coaching wins
giannis_questions: Most important offseason in franchise history
Front office weighing whether Giannis services best for team and superstar
Championship window concerns with aging roster

stories.headline: Rory Goes Back-to-Back: Fourth Player Ever to Win Consecutive Masters
stories.editorial_direction: Historic golf achievement. Joins Tiger, Jack, Faldo. Nearly blew 6-shot lead, parents finally got to see him win at Augusta.

tweets.text:
PMT 4-13 Masters Recap with @notthefakeSVP is Now Live! 

- Rory goes back to back at Augusta 
- Max and Brooks both top 12 
- NBA Playoffs are set
- Who's back of the week + More 

Watch or Listen Here http://beacons.ai/pardonmytake
.@notthefakeSVP joins @richeisen on SportsCenter to discuss Rory McIlroy's historic back-to-back Masters wins 🏆

historic_achievement: Fourth golfer ever to win consecutive Masters
Joins Jack Nicklaus (1965-66), Nick Faldo (1989-90), Tiger Woods (2001-02)
First to repeat since Tiger 22 years ago
the_drama: Nearly blew record 6-shot halfway lead on Saturday
Lost lead again Sunday before taking control on back nine
Won by one shot at 12-under, finished with bogey on 18
family_moment: Parents attended this year after missing last year's win
Almost didn't come because they thought not attending was lucky
Got to share green jacket ceremony with them this time
career_context: Sixth major championship, ties with Faldo for most by European in modern era
Completed career Grand Slam last year with first Masters
Waited decade between fifth and sixth majors
the_money: Won $4.5 million from record $22.5 million purse
Largest purse in major championship history
Beat Scottie Scheffler by one shot for victory
golf_significance: Now definitively in conversation among all-time greats
May never catch Nicklaus/Woods but pathway to No. 3 all-time
Raised floor so high that few brilliant shots put away field

stories.headline: Cooper Flagg Becomes First Rookie Since Jordan to Lead Team in Everything
stories.editorial_direction: Insane rookie stat. Led Mavs in points, rebounds, assists, and steals. Only MJ did this before.

tweets.text:
Cooper Flagg this season:

— Led Mavs in PTS
— Led Mavs in REB
— Led Mavs in AST
— Led Mavs in STL

The first rookie to lead his team in all four categories since MJ.

historic_stat: First rookie since Michael Jordan to lead team in PTS/REB/AST/STL
Did this for the Dallas Mavericks in his debut season
Incredibly rare for any player, let alone a rookie
jordan_parallel: MJ accomplished this feat early in career
Puts Flagg in exclusive company with GOAT
Shows complete impact beyond just scoring
what_it_means: Flagg was the most complete player on his team as a rookie
Demonstrates versatility across all aspects of game
Sets up monster sophomore campaign expectations

stories.headline: Kon Knueppel Beats LaMelo by One Three to Win NBA 3-Point Title
stories.editorial_direction: Insane race. Rookie beat established star by ONE three-pointer. First rookie to ever lead NBA in threes.

tweets.text:
KON WINS NBA 3PT RACE OVER LAMELO BY ONE THREE 🤯 

LaMelo Ball and Kon Knueppel are the first duo with 270+ threes each in a season since Steph and Klay 😳

Kon Knueppel is the first rookie in NBA history to lead the NBA in threes ☔️

Prior to this year, no rookie had even finished top 3!

the_race: Kon beat LaMelo by exactly one three-pointer for NBA lead
Both finished with 270+ threes, first duo since Steph/Klay
Came down to final games of regular season
rookie_record: First rookie in NBA history to lead league in threes
No rookie had even finished top 3 before this year
Historic shooting season from Duke product
context: LaMelo established as elite shooter, got beaten by rookie
Shows how prolific three-point shooting has become
Sets up rivalry between young stars going forward

stories.headline: Paul Reed Goes Perfect: 11-11 FG with 26 Points in Historic Performance
stories.editorial_direction: Perfect shooting game with elite stats. Only player in NBA history with 25/3 steals/3 blocks on 100% shooting.

tweets.text:
Paul Reed vs Indiana:

26 PTS
6 REB
3 STL
3 BLK
11-11 FG

Joins Jarrett Allen as the only players in NBA history with a 25/3s/3b game on 100 FG%.

perfect_game: Only second player ever with 25/3s/3b on perfect shooting
jarrett_allen_company: Joins Jarrett Allen as only players to achieve this feat
Shows elite two-way impact with perfect efficiency
Rare combination of offense and defense
end_of_season_gem: Perfect way to end regular season
Shows depth of talent across NBA

around_the_league.text:
This year's NFL draft risers: seven prospects thought to be climbing up draft boards, via @Jeff_Legwold:

https://www.espn.com/nfl/draft2026/story/_/id/48443025/2026-nfl-draft-risers-prospects-freeling-thieneman-iheanachor

Derik Queen vs Minnesota:

30 PTS
22 REB

The first rookie since Alonzo Mourning to record those numbers in a game.

Ryan Nembhard vs Chicago:

23 AST (!!)
15 PTS
9 REB
2 STL

The most AST by a rookie in a game since Ramon Sessions in 2008.

Really strong weekend for Texas.

Longhorns add Isaiah Johnson (Colorado) at PG and David Punch (TCU) at PF.

A pair of All-SEC caliber players in 2026-27.

"Houston is your first-round opponent…"

Deandre Ayton: "How do you know this?" 

"The league announced it."

DA: "When? I didn't get a chance to go look at it… You spoiled the surprise, bro." 😅

(via @mcten)

Deni Avdija vs Sacramento:

25 PTS
6 REB
10 AST

Portland secures the 8-seed and will face Phoenix in the Play-In for the 7-seed.

Shaq put the viral Pacers couple on the spot on LIVE TELEVISION 😭

"If you ask her to marry you right now, I'll buy the engagement ring."

Deandre Ayton vs Utah:

22 PTS
10 REB
3 BLK
10-14 FG

Ties AD for the fifth-most 20/10/3b games on 70+ FG% as a Laker.

I'M CRYING. The viral Pacers fan's reaction after going on Inside the NBA and Shaq tried to get her boyfriend to propose to her. 

"alright I agreed to do this so my boy could talk BASKETBALL"

"Am being told this show doesn't talk about basketball??" 😭😂

The only players to play 83 games this season:

— Mikal Bridges
— Keldon Johnson
— Julian Champagnie

Played all 82 and the NBA Cup Final.

closer.hook: Erik Spoelstra getting philosophical about the play-in tournament

tweets.text:
Erik Spoelstra on their confidence entering the play-in, citing familiarity 🗣️

"It is like the NCAA tournament. It's like FIBA. You know? It's harrowing. It's nuts… It makes you feel alive. That's for sure... Once you're in it, it's exhilarating. And you have to embrace the competition."

(via @ZachWeinberger)
